codeReview.summary: The feature renames three AppConnectionStepKind enum variants from Codex-specific to provider-generic names: FindingCodex → FindingAgent, InstallingCodex → InstallingAgent, StartingAppServer → StartingAgent. The changes are limited to two files (store/snapshot.rs for the enum definition and initial step construction, ffi/ssh.rs for all 44 usage sites including function bodies, comments, and test assertions). The UniFFI bindings were regenerated and correctly expose the renamed variants (Swift: findingAgent/installingAgent/startingAgent, Kotlin: FINDING_AGENT/INSTALLING_AGENT/STARTING_AGENT). Android's SnapshotExtensions.kt already references the new variant names. No old variant names remain anywhere in the Rust codebase. All 1250 Rust tests pass, cargo check and clippy are clean.

issues.description: Comment says 'This skips all Codex-specific bootstrap steps (FindingAgent, InstallingAgent, StartingAgent, OpeningTunnel)' — the step names are now generic but the comment still calls them 'Codex-specific'. The comment could be updated to say 'agent-specific' or 'provider-specific' for consistency with the rename intent, though this is cosmetic and does not affect functionality.

summary: Clean, well-scoped rename of AppConnectionStepKind variants from Codex-specific to provider-generic names. The worker followed the rust-backend-worker skill procedure correctly: verified baseline tests, performed systematic find-and-replace across all Rust references, updated comments and test assertion strings, regenerated UniFFI bindings, and ran full verification (cargo check, test, clippy). One minor cosmetic issue: a doc comment still calls the renamed steps 'Codex-specific' (line 1043 in ssh.rs), which is non-blocking. The Android Kotlin code already uses the new variant names, confirming binding regeneration was successful. No shared state gaps observed.
